name: Sopra Banking Software Core Banking API
what_it_does: Sopra Banking Software REST APIs for core banking, digital banking, and financial services platforms. Enables AI agents to manage customer account and deposit product data retrieval for digital banking automation, handle loan origination and credit lifecycle management for lending workflow automation, access payment processing and SEPA payment data for European payment automation, retrieve card issuance and card management data for payment card operations, handle digital onboarding and KYC workflow data for customer acquisition automation, access financial product catalog and pricing data for product management, retrieve regulatory reporting data including AnaCredit, FINREP, and Basel III compliance, manage account opening and servicing workflow data for banking operations, handle fund transfer and cross-border payment data for treasury management, and integrate core banking data with open banking platforms, fintech partnerships, and payment networks.
use_cases: Automating account servicing from digital banking agents
Processing loan applications from credit automation agents
Managing SEPA payments from European payment agents
Reporting regulatory data from compliance automation agents
not_for: US community banking without European core banking architecture
SMB banking without enterprise core banking platform complexity
Simple fintech without bank-grade regulatory and compliance requirements
best_when: A European bank, building society, or financial institution using Sopra Banking Software wants AI agents to automate core banking operations, digital customer onboarding, loan lifecycle management, SEPA payment processing, and regulatory compliance reporting.
avoid_when: REGULATORY RISK: Sopra Banking manages regulated banking data in PSD2, GDPR, and AnaCredit regulated environment — automated account actions without compliance validation creates banking regulatory violations. SEPA payment automation must comply with SEPA rulebooks and PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) requirements. AML and KYC automation must preserve human review for high-risk customer onboarding decisions. Core banking system changes have systemwide impact; automated changes require change management controls.

auth.notes: Sopra Banking Software uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. Per-bank credentials. Webhooks for banking events. REST API with JSON. Paris, France HQ. Subsidiary of Sopra Steria (CAC 40 company). 500+ bank clients globally. Strong in European retail and commercial banking. Sopra Banking Platform and Cassiopae (loan management) are primary products. Competes with Temenos, Finastra, and TCS BaNCS for European core banking market.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise license
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual license by institution size and module count
pricing.notes: Paris, France. Sopra Steria Group (CAC 40). 500+ bank clients in 80+ countries. European core banking: retail, commercial, corporate. Cassiopae loan management system. Strong in France, Spain, Belgium, and emerging markets. Open Banking Hub for PSD2 compliance. Competes with Temenos (Switzerland), Finastra (UK), and TCS BaNCS for European core banking.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth token; bank deployment dependent

known_agent_gotchas: REGULATORY RISK: PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) must be enforced for payment initiation; automated payment processing without SCA creates PSD2 compliance violations
AML/KYC automation limits — customer onboarding automation must preserve AML officer review for high-risk customers; automated KYC pass without human review creates money laundering compliance risk
European regulatory focus — Sopra Banking is EU-centric; non-European banking regulations and workflows may have limited coverage
AnaCredit regulatory reporting — European AnaCredit credit data reporting has strict ECB requirements; automated reporting must apply correct granular reporting logic
Core banking change risk — core banking system changes have systemwide impact; automated account and product configuration changes require formal change management approval
SEPA rulebook compliance — SEPA credit transfer and direct debit automation must comply with current SEPA rulebook versions; verify rulebook version applicability
agent_readiness.security_notes: Core banking. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, PSD2. OAuth2. EU/US. Banking account and regulatory data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Core banking. SOC2, GDPR, PSD2. OAuth2. EU. Banking account and payment data.
